title: Puerto Banus Luxury Catamaran Charter: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
For a lavish day afloat on the Costa del Sol, renting a luxury catamaran in Puerto Banus offers space, stability and style from around €5,000 for a full-day charter. Departing from Marina Banus, you can explore the Mediterranean coast with ease. BoatHire24 connects you with verified owners for a seamless booking experience.

How much does it cost to rent a luxury catamaran in Puerto Banus?

Full-day luxury catamaran charters in Puerto Banus typically range from €4,000 to €8,000 in 2026, depending on size and crew. A 50, 60 ft catamaran costs about €4,000, 5,500, while a 70 ft vessel like the Shangani Blue Lagoon can exceed €8,000. For those seeking a premium experience, exploring a yacht charter in Puerto Banus through BoatHire24 provides transparent pricing and direct owner contact.

Do you need a licence to rent a catamaran in Puerto Banus?

In Spain, a recreational navigation licence (PER) is legally required to skipper a bareboat catamaran over 15 hp or 6 metres. However, most luxury catamarans in Puerto Banus are crewed, so no licence is needed if you charter with a captain. Always confirm with the operator, as requirements can vary by boat size and itinerary.

Where to set off in Puerto Banus

The main departure point is Marina Banus, the glamorous port at the heart of Puerto Banus. Other nearby options include Puerto José Banus (part of the same complex) and the larger Puerto de la Duquesa, 20 minutes west. For a quieter start, consider Cabopino Marina or the historic port of Marbella, just 10 minutes east. Each offers easy access to the Mediterranean and shelter from the Poniente wind.

Which boat to rent

  1. Shangani Blue Lagoon 70 ft, ideal for large parties of up to 30 guests; full-day from €5,500. View the Shangani Blue Lagoon 70 ft charter page for details.
  2. Lagoon 620, perfect for families, sleeps 8, day charter from €4,000.
  3. Fountaine Pajot Saba 50, a balance of luxury and sailing performance, 10 guests, from €3,800.
  4. Azimut Flybridge 72, a motor yacht option, 12 guests, from €6,000.

Cost breakdown for catamaran rental in Puerto Banus

  • Shared tour on a catamaran: from €150 per person
  • Small motorboat (half-day, no licence): from €300
  • Catamaran full-day (50, 60 ft, crewed): from €4,000
  • Luxury crewed yacht (70 ft+): from €5,500

Seasons and weather tips

The high season in Puerto Banus runs from June to September, with July and August being the busiest. The Levante wind can pick up in the afternoon, often reaching 15, 20 knots. For calm conditions, book a morning charter.

To avoid the summer rush, charter in May or September when the sea is warm and the anchorages are quieter. The cala at Cabo Pino offers a sheltered stop 5 nm from Puerto Banus.

At a glance

  • Price band: €4,000, €8,000 per day for luxury catamaran
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Typical trip length: 8 hours (full-day)
  • Guest capacity: 10, 30 depending on boat
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Puerto Banus

Can I rent a catamaran for a sunset cruise in Puerto Banus?

Yes, many owners offer half-day sunset trips departing around 17:00 for three hours, costing from €2,500.

What is the best way to find a catamaran in Puerto Banus?

Use a trusted marketplace like BoatHire24 to compare verified listings, read reviews, and book directly with owners.

Are there catamarans suitable for large groups?

Yes, catamarans like the Shangani Blue Lagoon 70 ft can accommodate up to 30 guests for a party charter.

How far can I sail from Puerto Banus in a day?

A typical day trip covers 15, 25 nautical miles, reaching Sotogrande or Marbella’s coastline.
